Patch Set 4:

I attempted to fix the build errors present in this patch and succeeded. Yay! Now, keep in mind I barely have any idea what I am doing, so please doublecheck the changes I made to make sure they make sense.

This service is only provided in hopes to be useful and reduce latency from push to getting actual results in the face of trivial code errors

Here's summary of my changes:
The change in this patch addresses a compile error by correcting the format specifier for the `latency` field in a `printf` statement. Specifically:

- **Old format**: `latency %ld` — used `long` (`%ld`), which is incorrect for the `latency` field type.
- **New format**: `latency %llu` — uses `unsigned long long` (`%llu`), which matches the actual type of the `latency` field.

This fix ensures proper type matching in the `printf` format string, preventing compile warnings or errors related to format specifier mismatch. The rest of the output format remains unchanged.

parents.subject: LU-19323 tests: add more sanity-lnet subtests into IPv6 except list

commit.subject: LU-16832 lnet: add nano seconds delay latency support
commit.message:
LU-16832 lnet: add nano seconds delay latency support

Introduce nanosecond-resolution latency for LNet delay
fault injection. The previous interface accepted latency
only in seconds (u32). This change promotes the field to
u64 nanoseconds and plumbs it so that sub-second delays
(s/ms/us/ns) can be expressed precisely.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Sohei Koyama <skoyama@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I9ed1bd34cef0f0bf647f87d17dd68f05274e2b28
